title: Agricola Online
subtitle: Real-time multiplayer adaptation of the board game Agricola.
impact: Why it matters: a turn-based, room-per-match game is a genuine reason to reach for distributed systems — Go goroutines per game loop, WebSocket state sync, and Kubernetes that scales game servers with demand. Concurrency and orchestration done where they're actually needed.

title: Job Dashboard
subtitle: Self-hosted job tracker: Gmail in, Claude classification, native macOS widget out.
impact: Why it matters: a real end-to-end system — an LLM in the classification loop with structured output, an event-sourced data model that never regresses a stage, OAuth integrations (Gmail + Calendar), a background scheduler, and a native SwiftUI widget. The project that proves I can ship something complete.

title: Gym Coach App
subtitle: Adaptive personal-training companion built to be used daily.
impact: Why it matters: programming that adapts to real performance — progressive overload, deloads, missed-session handling — modelled as explicit logic rather than a static plan. A tool I actually use, which is why it keeps improving.

title: Embedded Handheld Console
subtitle: A pass-and-play game device running on custom embedded hardware.
impact: Why it matters: hardware + firmware + a turn-based game engine on constrained hardware — embedded/systems work most web developers never touch. Owned end-to-end, from the rules engine down to the boot sequence.
